title: 高功率计算的环境影响建模
background:
2024 HiMCM
Problem B: Examining the Environmental Impact of High-Powered Computing

As global demand for high-powered computing (HPC) continues to rise in sectors like artificial
intelligence (AI), data science and cryptocurrency mining, so does the environmental footprint of
these technologies. The increasing reliance on massive data centers, high performance
hardware, and energy-intensive computational processes has sparked concern about their
environmental consequences.

The most important aspect of the environmental impact of HPC is energy consumption and its
associated carbon emissions. This aspect stands out as it has a direct contribution to climate
change as energy consumption directly correlates with greenhouse gas emissions, particularly
when electricity is generated from fossil fuels. These HPC facilities often consume vast amounts
of energy, making their carbon footprint significant.  Additionally, the energy demand can strain
local power grids, especially in areas with limited renewable energy infrastructure leading to a
higher reliance on non-renewable energy sources.

In addition to energy consumption, there are other environmental concerns. The additional
environmental impacts of HPC are multi-faceted and can be categorized into several key areas:

•  Water Usage. Many data centers use water for cooling leading to substantial water

consumption and potential pollution with the discharge of their wastewater.

•  E-Waste. The manufacturing, use and disposal of computing hardware contributes to

electronic waste with many of the components being difficult to recycle.

•  Resource Depletion. The production of HPC hardware involves the extraction of rare
earth materials which can lead to habitat destruction, pollution, and additional demands
for energy.

•  Land Use. The physical space required for the data centers can lead to land use debates

with significant impacts on the local ecosystems.

•  Air Quality. This focuses on the emissions from fossil fuel power plants that can degrade
air quality and affect human health. Additional consideration should also be given to fine
particulate matter and dust generated by the data centers that impacts local air quality.
•  Chemical Use. The cooling systems use chemicals that can pose a risk if not managed

properly, including potential spills or leaks.

| ©2024 by COMAP | www.comap.org | www.mathmodels.org | info@comap.org |

 • Socioeconomic Impacts. Large data centers as well as the mining for resources can lead

to changes in the land with the potential for inequities in energy access for local
communities.

• Noise Pollution. All the operations involving machinery in every phase of this process

can contribute to increased noise pollution impacting communities and wildlife.

• Network Architecture. The network infrastructure needed for data transmission extends

beyond individual nodes to include greater connectivity concerns.

Many of these aspects have dedicated research into their environmental impacts that you can
research and use as background into understanding the problem.

Requirements.

1. Understand the Problem. Describe the scope of this problem in terms of the annual energy
consumption of the HPC capabilities worldwide considering both full capacity and average
utilization rates.

2. Create Your Model. Develop a comprehensive model to determine the environmental impact
of total carbon emissions resulting from energy consumption of HPC. Consideration should be
given to how the energy is produced, accounting for energy mixes.

3. Apply Your Model.

a. Explore how your model may change in the future with the growth of HPC, the

increasing demand for energy in other sectors and the potential for different energy
sources and mixes.

b. Use your model to provide realistic bounds that can provide insight into the scope of the

problem in the year 2030.

4. Expand Your Model and Reflect on Your Analysis.

a. Model the impact of increasing the portion of renewable energy and calculate the

corresponding reductions in carbon emissions. Investigate the effects of switching to a
100% renewable energy source as well as the potential challenges involved.

b. Refine your model (or develop a new model) to include the environmental impact of one
of the other key areas listed above to further understand the impact of HPC. Describe
why your group chose that aspect and how it relates to other key areas, especially energy
consumption.

5. Share Your Model and its Results.

a. Develop a set of actionable recommendations to reduce the environmental impact of

HPC, considering both technical and policy-oriented solutions.

b. Assume one of your recommendations is acted upon.  Determine and show how you can

incorporate this into your model.

| ©2024 by COMAP | www.comap.org | www.mathmodels.org | info@comap.org |

 c.  The United Nations Advisory Board issued a report on AI entitled “Governing AI for
Humanity” in September 2024 without significantly addressing HPC.[1] Write a one-to-
two-page letter to the Advisory Board urging them to include a more detailed section on
the environmental impacts of HPC in their scheduled developmental goals for 2030. Use
your findings and recommendations to support this plea.

Your PDF solution of no more than 25 total pages should include:

•  One-page Summary Sheet.
•  Table of Contents.
•  Your complete solution.
•  One- to two-page United Nations Advisory Board letter.
•  References list.
•  AI Use Report (If used does not count toward the 25-page limit.)

Note: There is no specific required minimum page length for a complete HiMCM submission.
You may use up to 25 total pages for all your solution work and any additional information you
want to include (for example: drawings, diagrams, calculations, tables). Partial solutions are
accepted. We permit the careful use of AI such as ChatGPT, although it is not necessary to create
a solution to this problem. If you choose to utilize a generative AI, you must follow the COMAP
AI use policy. This will result in an additional AI use report that you must add to the end of your
PDF solution file and does not count toward the 25 total page limit for your solution.

NEW HiMCM/MidMCM: Online Submission Process
The purpose of this article is to assist and guide students and advisors participating in
HiMCM/MidMCM. In the article, COMAP, provides information about the new online
submission process using the new online submission page
https://forms.comap.org/242386224483964. You will need your team's control number, advisor
id number and your problem choice to complete your submission.

Glossary

High-Powered Computing: Also known as high-performance computing (HPC), this refers to
the use of supercomputers and parallel processing techniques to solve complex computational
problems at high speeds.

E-Waste: Short for electronic waste, e-waste refers to discarded electrical or electronic devices.
This includes computers, smartphones, televisions, and appliances that are no longer in use. E-
waste is a significant environmental concern due to the hazardous materials it often contains.

Data Centers: Facilities used to house computer systems and associated components. Data
centers provide the infrastructure for processing, storing, and managing large volumes of data
and include servers, cooling systems, and backup power supplies to ensure reliable operation.

| ©2024 by COMAP | www.comap.org | www.mathmodels.org | info@comap.org |

 Node: A node in the context of network infrastructure is any device connected to a network that
can send, receive, or process data. It's like a junction point where data can be transmitted,
received, or modified.

Full Capacity: The maximum output that a power plant or energy source can produce under
optimal conditions. It is often expressed in megawatts (MW) or gigawatts (GW) and represents
the peak generation capability.

Average Utilization Rate: A measure of how effectively a power plant or energy source is
being used over a specified period. It is calculated by dividing the actual output by the full
capacity over the same period. This indicates how consistently a facility is operating compared to
its full potential.

Energy Mix: The combination of different energy sources used to meet energy needs. This can
include fossil fuels (coal, oil, natural gas), renewable sources (solar, wind, hydro, biomass), and
nuclear energy.

Fossil Fuels: Energy sources derived from ancient organic matter, primarily coal, oil, and natural
gas. These fuels are non-renewable and contribute to greenhouse gas emissions when burned.

Renewable Energy: Energy that comes from sources that naturally replenish themselves, such
as solar, wind, hydroelectric, geothermal, and biomass. These sources are generally considered
more sustainable and have a lower environmental impact.

Nuclear Energy: Energy produced through nuclear fission, where atomic nuclei are split to
release energy. It is a low-carbon source but involves concerns regarding radioactive waste and
nuclear safety.

References

[1] United Nations Advisory Board on AI (2024). Governing AI for Humanity.
https://sdgs.un.org/goals

Ahmed, M., & Verma, A. (2023). A review on the decarbonization of high-performance
computing centers. Journal of Cleaner Production. Retrieved from
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0959652623004567

Goldman Sachs. (2023). AI is poised to drive 160% increase in data center power demand.
Retrieved from https://www.goldmansachs.com

| ©2024 by COMAP | www.comap.org | www.mathmodels.org | info@comap.org |

steps.guide_style: 苏格拉底式追问，引导分点列举，不直接给答案
steps.deliverable: 子问题清单 + 关键约束
steps.title: 问题分析与重述
steps.prompt: 请阅读题目，用自己的话简要重述问题。重点说明：HPC的环境影响主要有哪些方面？本题要求我们建立什么模型？最终要输出哪些结果？

criteria.dim: 准确识别HPC环境影响的多个方面（能源、水、电子垃圾等）
清晰说明模型目标：评估碳排放及扩展至其他影响
列出最终输出：预测2030年碳排放、可再生能源影响、建议等

steps.reference_outline: HPC的环境影响包括能源消耗（碳排放）、水耗、电子垃圾、资源枯竭、土地利用、空气质量、化学品使用、社会经济影响、噪声污染和网络架构。本题要求建立模型评估HPC能源消耗产生的碳排放，考虑能源结构，预测2030年碳排放，并扩展至其他环境因素（如水耗），最后提出建议并撰写致联合国信函。
steps.hint: 注意题目中列出了9个方面的环境影响，但核心是能源消耗与碳排放。

steps.guide_style: 质疑每条假设的必要性与合理性
steps.title: 模型假设
steps.prompt: 列出至少5个合理的模型假设，并简要说明每个假设的理由。例如：假设全球HPC能源消耗数据可获取且准确；假设能源结构在未来按一定趋势变化等。

criteria.dim: 假设数量不少于5个
每个假设有合理的解释
假设与后续模型一致，不矛盾

steps.reference_outline: 1. 全球HPC能源消耗数据可基于公开报告估算。2. 能源结构（化石燃料、可再生能源比例）按历史趋势线性或指数变化。3. 碳排放因子（每单位能源的CO2排放）保持不变或按已知趋势变化。4. HPC计算能力增长与能源消耗增长成正比。5. 忽略地区差异，使用全球平均数据。
steps.hint: 考虑数据的可得性和模型的简化，例如假设全球平均能源结构。

steps.guide_style: 检查符号一致性与单位是否齐全
steps.title: 符号说明与变量定义
steps.prompt: 定义模型中使用的所有符号和变量。至少包括：$E(t)$（t年HPC能源消耗）、$C(t)$（t年碳排放）、$\rho(t)$（可再生能源比例）、$\alpha$（碳排放因子）等。用表格形式列出。

criteria.dim: 符号定义清晰，涵盖主要变量
使用表格形式，易于理解
符号与后续模型一致

steps.reference_outline:
| 符号 | 含义 | 单位 |
|------|------|------|
| $E(t)$ | 第t年全球HPC能源消耗 | MWh |
| $C(t)$ | 第t年HPC碳排放 | tCO2 |
| $\rho(t)$ | 第t年可再生能源占比 | 无量纲 |
| $\alpha_f$ | 化石燃料碳排放因子 | tCO2/MWh |
| $\alpha_r$ | 可再生能源碳排放因子 | tCO2/MWh |
| $r(t)$ | HPC能源消耗年增长率 | 无量纲 |
steps.hint: 碳排放因子可参考IPCC数据，化石燃料约0.5 tCO2/MWh，可再生能源约0.02 tCO2/MWh。

steps.guide_style: 逐步调试，关注可运行性与结果合理
steps.title: 模型建立：碳排放计算模型
steps.prompt: 建立计算HPC碳排放的数学模型。考虑能源结构，将总能源分为化石燃料和可再生能源两部分。写出碳排放$C(t)$关于$E(t)$和$\rho(t)$的表达式。

criteria.dim: 正确区分化石燃料和可再生能源
碳排放公式正确：$C(t) = E(t)[(1-\rho(t))\alpha_f + \rho(t)\alpha_r]$
说明公式中各项的含义

steps.reference_outline: 设总能源消耗为$E(t)$，可再生能源比例为$\rho(t)$，则化石燃料消耗为$E(t)(1-\rho(t))$，可再生能源消耗为$E(t)\rho(t)$。碳排放为两者之和：$C(t) = E(t)(1-\rho(t))\alpha_f + E(t)\rho(t)\alpha_r$。其中$\alpha_f$和$\alpha_r$分别为化石燃料和可再生能源的碳排放因子。
steps.hint: 注意可再生能源也有少量碳排放（如制造过程），但通常远低于化石燃料。

steps.guide_style: 逐步调试，关注可运行性与结果合理
steps.title: 模型求解：预测2030年碳排放
steps.prompt: 假设$E(t)$和$\rho(t)$已知（或给出具体函数形式），计算2030年的碳排放。例如，设$E(t)=E_0(1+r)^t$，$\rho(t)=\rho_0+kt$，取合理参数值（如$E_0=2\times10^8$ MWh，$r=0.05$，$\rho_0=0.2$，$k=0.01$，$\alpha_f=0.5$，$\alpha_r=0.02$），计算$C(2030)$。

criteria.dim: 正确代入参数计算
计算过程清晰，单位正确
给出最终数值结果

steps.hint: 注意指数增长的计算，可使用计算器或编程。

steps.guide_style: 追问结果是否可信、与现实是否吻合
steps.title: 结果分析与检验
steps.prompt: 对上述计算结果进行分析：与当前全球碳排放（约50亿吨）比较，占比多少？讨论模型的合理性和局限性。

criteria.dim: 正确计算占比并解释
分析模型合理性（如参数选择是否合理）
指出局限性（如未考虑技术进步、政策变化等）

steps.reference_outline: 计算得2030年HPC碳排放约1.005亿吨，占全球碳排放（假设50亿吨）约0.2%。模型合理性：参数基于历史趋势，但实际增长率可能更高（如AI发展）。局限性：未考虑能源效率提升、政策变化（如碳中和目标）以及地区差异。
steps.hint: 可参考IPCC或IEA的全球碳排放数据。

steps.guide_style: 引导设计扰动实验并解读
steps.title: 灵敏度分析
steps.prompt: 选择两个关键参数（如增长率$r$和可再生能源比例增长率$k$），分别变化±10%，重新计算2030年碳排放，并分析结果对参数的敏感程度。

criteria.dim: 正确进行参数变化计算
计算灵敏度指标（如百分比变化）
分析哪个参数更敏感

steps.hint: 灵敏度可计算为（新值-原值）/原值/参数变化比例。

steps.guide_style: 对照假设找局限，避免空泛
steps.title: 模型评价：优缺点
steps.prompt: 总结你的模型的优点和缺点。至少各列出3点。

criteria.dim: 优点不少于3条，具体且合理
缺点不少于3条，具体且合理

steps.reference_outline: 优点：1. 模型简单易懂，易于计算。2. 考虑了能源结构，更贴近实际。3. 可扩展至其他环境因素。缺点：1. 假设能源结构线性变化，可能不准确。2. 未考虑技术进步带来的能效提升。3. 使用全球平均数据，忽略地区差异。
steps.hint: 从假设、数据、模型结构等方面思考。

steps.guide_style: 强调创新点与清晰表达
steps.title: 模型推广与论文写作要点
steps.prompt: 提出至少两种模型推广方向（如加入水耗、电子垃圾等），并简要说明如何修改模型。同时，列出论文写作中需要包含的关键部分（如摘要、假设、模型、结果、灵敏度、结论等）。

criteria.dim: 推广方向具体且可行
说明修改方法
论文结构完整，符合HiMCM要求

steps.reference_outline: 推广方向：1. 加入水耗：建立水耗与能源消耗的关系，如冷却水用量与能源消耗成正比。2. 加入电子垃圾：考虑硬件更新周期，建立电子垃圾产生量模型。论文结构：摘要、问题重述、假设、符号、模型建立、求解、结果分析、灵敏度、模型评价、推广、建议、参考文献、附录（含代码）。
steps.hint: 参考获奖论文的章节安排。
